content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA with NNN **positive** elements A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You are also given a function SSS on any array CCC with length NNN defined as follow:

S(C)S(C)S(C) = ∑i=1N(Ci−1+Ci+12−Ci)\sum\limits_{i=1}^{N}(\dfrac{C_{i-1} + C_{i+1}}{2}-C_i)i=1∑N​(2Ci−1​+Ci+1​​−Ci​)

**Note** that conventionally, we define all elements outside the range of the array to have value 000. In this context, C0=CN+1=0C_0 = C_{N + 1} = 0C0​=CN+1​=0.

Consider the following process:


Choose a permutation PPP of values from 111 to NNN uniformly randomly.
Let BBB be the array of NNN elements B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, where Bi=APiB_i = A_{P_i}Bi​=APi​​.
Define VVV as the expected value of ∣S(B)∣|S(B)|∣S(B)∣. Find ⌊V⌋\lfloor V \rfloor⌊V⌋.

**Note** :


∣X∣|X|∣X∣ denotes absolute value of XXX
⌊X⌋\lfloor X \rfloor⌊X⌋ is the greatest integer that does not exceed XXX.

Input Format

The first line of the input contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\dots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the elements of the array.


Output Format
For each testcase, output ⌊V⌋\lfloor V \rfloor⌊V⌋ in a new line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing floor of the expected value V, which equals floor of the average of the given numbers.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case sum=2, N=2, average=1 → floor=1. For the second test case sum=12, N=3, average=4 → floor=4.
Only one element, average equals the element itself.

solution.approach: For a permutation B of A we have S(B) = -(B1 + BN)/2, therefore |S(B)| = (B1 + BN)/2. Because the permutation is uniform, each element of A appears at position 1 with probability 1/N and also at position N with probability 1/N. By linearity of expectation, E[B1] = E[BN] = (sum of A)/N. Hence V = (1/2)*(E[B1] + E[BN]) = (sum of A)/N. The required answer is floor(V), i.e., integer division of the total sum by N. The algorithm just computes the sum of the array using 64‑bit integer and prints sum/N for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O( Σ N ) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        s = 0
        for _ in range(N):
            s += next(it)
        out.append(str(s // N))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
